metadata.title: Jurisdiction Divergence Correction Metrics
metadata.scope: All 151 divergences across 5 source files in the jurisdiction training data

overall_accuracy.note: Accuracy rate is the proportion of claims that are fully confirmed correct. The 3 false divergences represent 2.0% of total claims — concentrated entirely in the Bioequivalence domain.

false_divergences_corrected.root_cause: Failure to incorporate ICH M9 (Nov 2019) harmonization into training data updates. Additionally one factual error about FDA RSABE scope (not attributable to M9 — this was an inaccuracy in the original data).

items.original_error: FDA: Class I only; EMA: Class I AND III; PMDA: case-by-case
items.correct_position: ALL three jurisdictions accept Class I AND Class III per ICH M9 (FDA: May 2021; EMA: Nov 2019; PMDA: Nov 2019)
items.client_financial_risk: $500K-$2M per unnecessary in vivo BE study
items.regulatory_basis: ICH M9 Step 4 Nov 2019; FDA Guidance May 2021
items.adversarial_test_result: PASSED — no ICH region still restricts to Class I only post-M9

items.topic: BCS biowaivers — positions reversed AND wrong
items.original_error: EU: Class I only; FDA: Class I AND Class III (reversed from v2 file, and both wrong)
items.correct_position: Both EU and FDA accept Class I AND Class III per ICH M9
items.client_financial_risk: $500K-$2M per unnecessary in vivo BE study
items.regulatory_basis: ICH M9 Step 4 Nov 2019; FDA Guidance May 2021; EMA 2010 Bioequivalence guideline Appendix III
items.adversarial_test_result: PASSED — EU accepted Class III before M9; FDA accepted Class III from May 2021

items.original_error: FDA RSABE allowed for BOTH AUC and Cmax
items.correct_position: FDA RSABE is for Cmax ONLY. AUC remains 80-125% in both FDA and EMA. The claimed divergence (FDA allows AUC widening; EMA does not) does not exist — both restrict AUC to 80-125%.
items.client_financial_risk: Undersized BE study; failed regulatory submission; repeat study $1-5M; 12-24 month delay
items.regulatory_basis: EMA Guideline Section 4.1.10 (explicit); FDA RSABE guidance (implicit — Cmax-only application)
items.adversarial_test_result: PASSED — no FDA guidance document allows RSABE for AUC in standard HVD context

needs_clarification_items.action_priority: Medium — these are not false divergences but contain inaccuracies of fact, precision, or completeness

highest_priority_items.issue: FDA batch record retention described as two-option rule; actually 1-year-after-expiry as primary rule
Internal inconsistency — body text says '8 years' but summary says CUSMA-aligned 6+8+0.5 structure
OCT1 transporter divergence overstated; FDA 2020 DDI guidance update narrowed gap
highest_priority_items.action: Add note that this is an evolving area and recharacterize as 'narrowing divergence'

cannot_verify_due_to: PAL/PMD Act English translation not in source document collection (Japan)
PMDA DDI guideline (2019) not in source collection (Japan)
Health Canada Food and Drug Regulations — deep sections inaccessible due to PDF size (Canada)
cannot_verify_items.risk_assessment: LOW to MEDIUM — the unverifiable claims are consistent with well-established regulatory practice. The two Japan items (JP-001, JP-011) are low-risk: JP-001 is established PMDA practice; JP-011 is historical fact. The three Canada items (DIV-CA-002, DIV-CA-003, DIV-CA-011) are plausible FDR section references that should be verified when the full FDR text is accessible.
cannot_verify_items.recommended_action: Add PAL/PMD Act English translation and PMDA DDI 2019 guideline to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/. Extract C.05, C.08, C.01A sections from Health Canada FDR PDF into separate searchable files.

domain_accuracy_breakdown.description: Accuracy rates by regulatory domain across all source files

domains.note: Lowest accuracy domain. All 3 false divergences are here. Root cause: ICH M9 harmonization not incorporated and one factual RSABE error.
High accuracy. EU GMP Annex 1 divergences well-documented with specific section citations.
High accuracy. Legal basis citations verified against Dir 2001/83/EC and Reg 726/2004.
Accuracy limited by unavailability of PAL/PMD Act and PMDA DDI guideline in source documents. No false divergences found.
Accuracy limited by inaccessibility of deep FDR sections and one internal data inconsistency (CA-006). No false divergences found.

items_requiring_ongoing_monitoring.monitoring_reason: Japan has been evolving toward narrative-based descriptions. Verify PMDA pregnancy labeling requirements annually.
FDA 2020 DDI guidance included OCT1. This divergence is narrowing. Re-evaluate with each FDA/PMDA DDI guidance update.

changes: DIV-004: Complete rewrite of BCS biowaiver divergence to reflect ICH M9 harmonization. Change divergence_level from 'significant' to 'minor'. Update summary_matrix row.
DIV-017: Minor wording change — remove 'longest' superlative for Japan; replace with 'tied for longest with EU at 10 years'

DIV-016: Complete rewrite. Split into three divergences (DIV-016a reference product, DIV-016b RSABE methodology, DIV-016c BCS biowaivers). Correct BCS positions. Remove RSABE-for-AUC claim. Reduce DIV-016c from 'critical' to 'minor' severity.
DIV-011: Correct FDA retention description to match 21 CFR 211.180(a) — 1 year after expiry; not a two-option rule for standard products.
DIV-012: Change 'annual' PQR to 'periodic or rolling (typically annual)'
DIV-CA-006: Reconcile body text and summary_statistics. Update to CUSMA-aligned 6+8+0.5 structure.
DIV-CA-001: Add clarification that 300-day target is a service standard, not a statutory requirement.

quality_gates.description: Minimum quality thresholds that should be met before using jurisdiction divergence training data

gates.status: FAILING before correction / PASSING after correction
gates.details: 2 CRITICAL false divergences existed (FALSE-001, FALSE-002). Both corrected in corrected-divergences.json.

gates.status: FAILING before correction / PASSING after correction
gates.details: 1 HIGH false divergence existed (FALSE-003). Corrected in corrected-divergences.json.

gates.gate: BCS biowaiver position consistent across all files
gates.status: FAILING before correction / PASSING after correction
gates.details: v2 and v3 files directly contradicted each other AND both were wrong. Both corrected to ICH M9 harmonized position.

gates.status: FAILING before correction / PASSING after correction
gates.details: RSABE-for-AUC claim removed. Both FDA and EMA now correctly described as Cmax-only.

gates.gate: No pre-2020 superseded positions in Bioequivalence domain
gates.status: FAILING before correction / PASSING after correction
PASSING (83.4% confirmed, higher when excluding unverifiable items)
gates.details: Meets threshold. Target for next verification cycle: >= 90% with additional source documents.

triggers_for_earlier_review: FDA or EMA publishes new bioequivalence guidance
Health Canada regulatory modernization initiative affecting FDR
Any jurisdiction formally changes BCS biowaiver criteria
high_priority_gaps_to_close: Add PAL/PMD Act English translation to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/ to enable verification of JP-001
Add PMDA DDI guideline (2019) to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/ for JP-009, JP-011, JP-017
Extract Health Canada FDR sections C.05, C.08, C.01A into searchable sub-files
Add PM(NOC) Regulations to stage-1/health-canada/ for patent linkage verification
